title: Intricately Snappy Dutch Jerusalem Artichokes Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Dutch stew featuring Jerusalem artichokes, made with katsuramuki-cut vegetables and cooked to perfection in a bamboo steamer, this dish is not only delicious but also low in calories.
instructions: In a large pan, melt the butter over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until softened.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the katsuramuki-cut Jerusalem artichokes, chopped carrots, and chopped celery to the pan, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the vegetable broth, and add the dried thyme, salt, and black pepper, stirring to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender.
While the stew is cooking, prepare the bamboo steamer by filling the bottom with water to a depth of about 1 inch.
Once the stew has finished cooking, carefully pour it into the bamboo steamer, and cover with a lid.
Steam the stew over boiling water for an additional 10 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the vegetables are heated through.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
